texto: Misery is a scar on the soul, that if it begins in childhood, it lasts the whole lifetime. I understand that no two scars are alike, but I also ask myself; even if these scars are not alike, aren’t these things engraved on our souls signed by which we know each other?Aren’t we also alike?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Bahaa Taher que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “Misery is a scar on the soul, that if it begins in childhood, it lasts the whole lifetime. I unde…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, childhood, depression, com foco em depression e memories.
